seoTitle: Build a Video Editor Portfolio Website on Wix
metaDescription: Learn how to design a video editing portfolio on Wix with showreel galleries, project grids, and client testimonials.
intro: A strong video editing portfolio needs to showcase your reel prominently while making it easy for potential clients to browse individual projects. This guide covers setting up a video-focused Wix layout, embedding showreels, organizing project galleries, and adding the contact elements that turn viewers into paying clients.

steps.heading: Choose a portfolio-friendly layout
steps.body: Select a Wix design framework suited to displaying media-heavy creative work.

steps.heading: Add your main showreel
steps.body: Deploy a primary video strip component to feature your best editing work at the top of the page.

steps.heading: Organize project galleries
steps.body: Insert grid galleries to separate different project categories or client work.

steps.heading: Embed your video links
steps.body: Use the Wix Video app or embed code to pull in high-quality video from YouTube or Vimeo.

steps.heading: Add project details and testimonials
steps.body: Include text describing your role, software used, and client feedback alongside each project.

steps.heading: Add a contact call to action and preview
steps.body: Include a booking or contact form, check mobile responsiveness, and preview playback before publishing.

faq.question: Should I host video files directly or embed from YouTube?
faq.answer: Embedding from platforms like YouTube or Vimeo is generally lighter on page load times than hosting large video files directly.

faq.question: How many projects should I feature on my portfolio?
faq.answer: There's no fixed number, but keeping it focused on your strongest, most relevant work tends to make a stronger impression than an exhaustive list.

faq.question: Can I add client testimonials next to my video work?
faq.answer: Yes, adding text blocks near your project galleries is a good way to pair feedback directly with the relevant work.

faq.question: Will my videos play correctly on mobile?
faq.answer: Check the responsive mobile view and test playback there specifically, since video scaling can behave differently than on desktop.
